steps.title: Combine the functions f(x) and g(x)
steps.content:
Substitute the functions into $h(x) = f(x) + g(x)$:
$$h(x) = \frac{2}{5}x - 8 + \frac{3}{5}x + 28$$
Combine the $x$-terms: $\frac{2}{5}x + \frac{3}{5}x = x$
Combine the constants: $-8 + 28 = 20$
So $h(x) = x + 20$

steps.title: Set h(x) = 0 and solve for x
steps.content:
The $x$-intercept occurs where $y = 0$:
$$0 = x + 20$$
$$x = -20$$
The $x$-intercept is at $(-20, 0)$. That confirms B is correct.
